devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । एकैकशस्ताः कृष्णस्य पुत्रान्दश दशाबलाः । अजीजनन्ननवमान्पितुः सर्वात्मसम्पदा ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca ekaikaśastāḥ kṛṣṇasya putrāndaśa daśābalāḥ ajījanannanavamānpituḥ sarvātmasampadā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Rukmī who was humiliated by Kṛṣṇa (by deforming him after defeat) continued to (brood over and) nurse the enmity. By his penance, he propitiated god Śiva abiding on mount Śrī Śaila.

devanagari: गृहादनपगं वीक्ष्य राजपुत्र्योऽच्युतं स्थितम् । प्रेष्ठं न्यमंसत स्वं स्वं न तत्तत्त्वविदः स्त्रियः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: gṛhādanapagaṃ vīkṣya rājaputryo preṣṭhaṃ nyamaṃsata svaṃ svaṃ na tattattvavidaḥ striyaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sthā class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sthā class 1 verb]

translation: Rudra, the most prominent among boon-conferring gods, was propitiated by Rukmī by performance of penance. He asked Rukmī to seek a boon. He sought for a strong unbreakable bow which will be invincible for hosts of enemies and unsunderable even to gods. “So be it”, said the god Śiva and gave him the bow. But god Śaṅkara cautioned him thus:

devanagari: चार्वब्जकोशवदनायतबाहुनेत्र । सप्रेमहासरसवीक्षितवल्गुजल्पैः । सम्मोहिता भगवतो न मनो विजेतुं । स्वैर्विभ्रमैः समशकन्वनिता विभूम्नः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: cārvabjakośavadanāyatabāhunetra sapremahāsarasavīkṣitavalgujalpaiḥ sammohitā bhagavato na mano vijetuṃ svairvibhramaiḥ samaśakanvanitā vibhūmnaḥ
translation: Rudra, the most prominent among boon-conferring gods, was propitiated by Rukmī by performance of penance. He asked Rukmī to seek a boon. He sought for a strong unbreakable bow which will be invincible for hosts of enemies and unsunderable even to gods. “So be it”, said the god Śiva and gave him the bow. But god Śaṅkara cautioned him thus:

devanagari: स्मायावलोकलवदर्शितभावहारि । भ्रूमण्डलप्रहितसौरतमन्त्रशौण्डैः । पत्न्यस्तु षोडशसहस्रमनङ्गबाणैर् । यस्येन्द्रियं विमथितुं करणैर्न शेकुः ॥...
english_devnagari: smāyāvalokalavadarśitabhāvahāri bhrūmaṇḍalaprahitasauratamantraśauṇḍaiḥ patnyastu ṣoḍaśasahasramanaṅgabāṇair yasyendriyaṃ vimathituṃ karaṇairna śekuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present passive first single], [imperative active second single]

translation: “When you confront god Viṣṇu, this bow will desert you and will return to me. Not otherwise.”

devanagari: इत्थं रमापतिमवाप्य पतिं स्त्रियस्ता । ब्रह्मादयोऽपि न विदुः पदवीं यदीयाम् । भेजुर्मुदाविरतमेधितयानुराग । हासावलोकनवसङ्गमलालसाद्यम् ॥ ५ ...
english_devnagari: itthaṃ ramāpatimavāpya patiṃ striyastā brahmādayo bhejurmudāviratamedhitayānurāga hāsāvalokanavasaṅgamalālasādyam
translation: Telling him thus, god Śiva gave him the bow and disappeared. Having secured the boon, the king returned to his capital Bhojakaṭa.

devanagari: प्रत्युद्गमासनवरार्हणपादशौच । ताम्बूलविश्रमणवीजनगन्धमाल्यैः । केशप्रसारशयनस्नपनोपहार्यैः । दासीशता अपि विभोर्विदधुः स्म दास्यम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: pratyudgamāsanavarārhaṇapādaśauca tāmbūlaviśramaṇavījanagandhamālyaiḥ keśaprasāraśayanasnapanopahāryaiḥ dāsīśatā api vibhorvidadhuḥ sma dāsyam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ das ], [accusative single from √ das ]

translation: With the help of the bow, Rukmī brought under his control all kings noted for their prowess in battle. Kṛṣṇa was the only exception.

devanagari: तासां या दशपुत्राणां कृष्णस्त्रीणां पुरोदिताः । अष्टौ महिष्यस्तत्पुत्रान्प्रद्युम्नादीन्गृणामि ते ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: tāsāṃ yā daśaputrāṇāṃ kṛṣṇastrīṇāṃ puroditāḥ aṣṭau mahiṣyastatputrānpradyumnādīngṛṇāmi te

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vad class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vad class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ aś class 5 verb], [vocative dual from √ aś class 5 verb], [accusative dual from √ aś class 5 verb]

translation: With greed of retaining the possession of the bow, Rukmī did not even think of opposing Vāsudeva. Nor did he think of entering into faithful alliance with him, O great king.

devanagari: चारुदेष्णः सुदेष्णश्च चारुदेहश्च वीर्यवान् । सुचारुश्चारुगुप्तश्च भद्रचारुस्तथापरः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: cārudeṣṇaḥ sudeṣṇaśca cārudehaśca vīryavān sucāruścāruguptaśca bhadracārustathāparaḥ
translation: Although he still cherished the enmity, he remembered the words of god Śiva. Pradyumna who was the veritable Manmatha (the god of Love) incarnate, was chosen by Rukmavatī i n the Svayaṃvara.

devanagari: चारुचन्द्रो विचारुश्च चारुश्च दशमो हरेः । प्रद्युम्नप्रमुखा जाता रुक्मिण्यां नावमाः पितुः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: cārucandro vicāruśca cāruśca daśamo hareḥ pradyumnapramukhā jātā rukmiṇyāṃ nāvamāḥ pituḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ jan class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ jan class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ jan class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ jan class 2 verb], [nominative plural from √ jan class 3 verb], [vocative plural from √ jan class 3 verb], [nominative plural from √ jan class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ jan class 4 verb]

[nominative plural from √ jan class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ jan class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ jan class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ jan class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ jan class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ jan class 2 verb], [nominative plural from √ jan class 3 verb], [vocative plural from √ jan class 3 verb], [accusative plural from √ jan class 3 verb], [nominative plural from √ jan class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ jan class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ jan class 4 verb]

translation: Having vanquished in the battle, all the kings assembled there (for the svayaṃvara) single-handedly in his chariot, he carried her away. The great warrior Aniruddha was born of Rukmavatī.

devanagari: भानुः सुभानुः स्वर्भानुः प्रभानुर्भानुमांस्तथा । चन्द्र भानुर्बृहद्भानुरतिभानुस्तथाष्टमः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: bhānuḥ subhānuḥ svarbhānuḥ prabhānurbhānumāṃstathā candra bhānurbṛhadbhānuratibhānustathāṣṭamaḥ
translation: Being directed by (Uṣā) Bāṇa’s daughter, Citralekhā kidnapped him; and on account of him, the thousand-armed Bāṇa became a two-armed one.

devanagari: श्रीभानुः प्रतिभानुश्च सत्यभामात्मजा दश । साम्बः सुमित्रः पुरुजिच्छतजिच्च सहस्रजित् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhānuḥ pratibhānuśca satyabhāmātmajā daśa sāmbaḥ sumitraḥ purujicchatajicca sahasrajit
translation: Bhānu, Subhānu, Svarbhānu, Prabhānu, Bhānumān, Candrabhānu, Bṛhadbhānu, and the eighth Atibhānu, Śrībhānu, Pratibhānu—these were the ten sons by Satyabhāmā. Sāmba, Sumitra, Purujit, Śatajit, Sahasrajit, Vijaya, Citraketu, Vasumān. Dravid, Kratu—these were the ten sons of Jāmbavatī. Sāmba and these others were all favourites of their Father.

devanagari: विजयश्चित्रकेतुश्च वसुमान्द्रविडः क्रतुः । जाम्बवत्याः सुता ह्येते साम्बाद्याः पितृसम्मताः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: vijayaścitraketuśca vasumāndraviḍaḥ kratuḥ jāmbavatyāḥ sutā hyete sāmbādyāḥ pitṛsammatāḥ

translation: Bhānu, Subhānu, Svarbhānu, Prabhānu, Bhānumān, Candrabhānu, Bṛhadbhānu, and the eighth Atibhānu, Śrībhānu, Pratibhānu—these were the ten sons by Satyabhāmā. Sāmba, Sumitra, Purujit, Śatajit, Sahasrajit, Vijaya, Citraketu, Vasumān. Dravid, Kratu—these were the ten sons of Jāmbavatī. Sāmba and these others were all favourites of their Father.

devanagari: वीरश्चन्द्रो ऽश्वसेनश्च चित्रगुर्वेगवान्वृषः । आमः शङ्कुर्वसुः श्रीमान्कुन्तिर्नाग्नजितेः सुताः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: vīraścandro āmaḥ śaṅkurvasuḥ śrīmānkuntirnāgnajiteḥ sutāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ su class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ su class 5 verb]

[nominative plural from √ sū class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ sū class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ sū class 2 verb]

translation: Vīra (v.l. Bhānu), Candra, Aśvasena, Citragu, Vegavān Vṛṣa, Āma, Śaṅku. Vasu, Śrīmān, Kunti were the sons of Nagnajitī.

devanagari: श्रुतः कविर्वृषो वीरः सुबाहुर्भद्र एकलः । शान्तिर्दर्शः पूर्णमासः कालिन्द्याः सोमकोऽवरः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: śrutaḥ kavirvṛṣo vīraḥ subāhurbhadra ekalaḥ śāntirdarśaḥ pūrṇamāsaḥ kālindyāḥ somako
translation: Śruta, Kavi, Vṛṣa, Vīra, Subāhu, Bhadra, Ekala, Śānti, Darśa, Pūrṇamāsa, Avara and Somaka were the ten sons of Kālindī.

devanagari: प्रघोषो गात्रवान्सिंहो बलः प्रबल ऊर्ध्वगः । माद्र्याः पुत्रा महाशक्तिः सह ओजोऽपराजितः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: praghoṣo gātravānsiṃho balaḥ prabala ūrdhvagaḥ mādryāḥ putrā mahāśaktiḥ saha ojo
translation: Praghoṣa (v.l. Prajaṅgha), Gātravān, Siṃha, Bala, Prabala, Ūrdhvaga, Mahāśakti, Sahas, Ojas and Aparājita were the sons of Mādrī (Lakṣmaṇā).

devanagari: वृको हर्षोऽनिलो गृध्रो वर्धनोऽन्नाद एव च । महाशः पावनो वह्निर्मित्रविन्दात्मजाः क्षुधिः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: vṛko harṣo mahāśaḥ pāvano vahnirmitravindātmajāḥ kṣudhiḥ
translation: Vṛka, Harṣa, Anila, Gṛdhra, Vardhana and Annāda, as also Mahāṃśa, Pavana, Vahni and Kṣudhi were the sons of Mitravindā.

devanagari: सङ्ग्रामजिद्बृहत्सेनः शूरः प्रहरणोऽरिजित् । जयः सुभद्रो भद्राया वाम आयुश्च सत्यकः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: saṅgrāmajidbṛhatsenaḥ śūraḥ praharaṇo jayaḥ subhadro bhadrāyā vāma āyuśca satyakaḥ
translation: Saṅgrāmajit, Bṛhatsena, Śūra, Praharaṇa, Arijit, Jaya, Subhadra, Vāma, Āyus and Satyaka were the sons of Bhadrā.[2]
purport:
Footnote [2]:
It appears a slip on the part of Bhāvāratha Dīpikā to equate her with Śaibyā-VT.

devanagari: दीप्तिमांस्ताम्रतप्ताद्या रोहिण्यास्तनया हरेः । प्रद्युम्नाच्चानिरुद्धोऽभूद्रुक्मवत्यां महाबलः । पुत्र्यां तु रुक्मिणो राजन्नाम्ना भोज...
english_devnagari: dīptimāṃstāmrataptādyā rohiṇyāstanayā hareḥ pradyumnāccāniruddho putryāṃ tu rukmiṇo rājannāmnā bhojakaṭe pure
translation: Dīptimān, Tāmratapta and others were born to Hari by Rohiṇī.[3] (She was the chief of the group of sixteen thousand and one hundred queens). A mighty son Aniruddha was born of Pradyumna by Rukmavatī the Princess of Rukmī in the city of Bhojakaṭa (while Pradyumna was there).
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Obviously a similarity of name in the women of the same family.

devanagari: एतेषां पुत्रपौत्राश्च बभूवुः कोटिशो नृप । मातरः कृष्णजातीनां सहस्राणि च षोडश ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: eteṣāṃ putrapautrāśca babhūvuḥ koṭiśo nṛpa mātaraḥ kṛṣṇajātīnāṃ sahasrāṇi ca ṣoḍaśa
translation:
Dīptimān, Tāmratapta and others were born to Hari by Rohiṇī.[3] (She was the chief of the group of sixteen thousand and one hundred queens). A mighty son Aniruddha was born of Pradyumna by Rukmavatī the Princess of Rukmī in the city of Bhojakaṭa (while Pradyumna was there).
As the mothers of Kṛṣṇa’s sons were sixteen thousand in number, the sons and grandsons of those sons numbered billions.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Obviously a similarity of name in the women of the same family.

devanagari: श्रीराजोवाच । कथं रुक्म्यरिपुत्राय प्रादाद्दुहितरं युधि । कृष्णेन परिभूतस्तं हन्तुं रन्ध्रं प्रतीक्षते । एतदाख्याहि मे विद्वन्द्विषोर्...
english_devnagari: śrīrājovāca kathaṃ rukmyariputrāya prādādduhitaraṃ yudhi kṛṣṇena paribhūtastaṃ hantuṃ randhraṃ pratīkṣate etadākhyāhi me vidvandviṣorvaivāhikaṃ mithaḥ

[nominative single from √ prā class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ prā class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ prā class 2 verb]

translation:
King Parīkṣit enquired:
Oh learned sage! How is it that Rukmī who was vanquished in combat by Kṛṣṇa and was eagerly waiting for an opportunity to avenge, gave his daughter in marriage to his enemy’s son? Be pleased to narrate to me how the matrimonial alliance came about between these two deadly enemies.

devanagari: अनागतमतीतं च वर्तमानमतीन्द्रियम् । विप्रकृष्टं व्यवहितं सम्यक्पश्यन्ति योगिनः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: anāgatamatītaṃ ca vartamānamatīndriyam viprakṛṣṭaṃ vyavahitaṃ samyakpaśyanti yoginaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vṛt class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ vṛt class 1 verb]

translation: For the yogins can accurately and distinctly see the future, the past and the present as well as what is beyond the ken of senses, which is very remote and screened off (by something else).

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । वृतः स्वयंवरे साक्षादनङ्गोऽङ्गयुतस्तया । राज्ञः समेतान्निर्जित्य जहारैकरथो युधि ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca vṛtaḥ svayaṃvare sākṣādanaṅgo rājñaḥ sametānnirjitya jahāraikaratho yudhi

translation:
Śrī Śuka narrated:
Being the actual embodiment of Anaṅga (the god of Love), he (Pradyumna) was selected by Rukmavatī in her svayaṃvara (self-election by the bride-type of marriage). Completely defeating the assembled kings single-handedly in his chariot, he carried her away.

devanagari: यद्यप्यनुस्मरन्वैरं रुक्मी कृष्णावमानितः । व्यतरद्भागिनेयाय सुतां कुर्वन्स्वसुः प्रियम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: yadyapyanusmaranvairaṃ rukmī kṛṣṇāvamānitaḥ vyataradbhāgineyāya sutāṃ kurvansvasuḥ priyam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]
[nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: Being directed by (Uṣā) Bāṇa’s daughter, Citralekhā kidnapped him; and on account of him, the thousand-armed Bāṇa became a two-armed one.

devanagari: रुक्मिण्यास्तनयां राजन्कृतवर्मसुतो बली । उपयेमे विशालाक्षीं कन्यां चारुमतीं किल ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: rukmiṇyāstanayāṃ rājankṛtavarmasuto balī upayeme viśālākṣīṃ kanyāṃ cārumatīṃ kila

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: It is reported that the mighty son of Kṛtavarmā married Cārumatī, the large (beautiful) eyed daughter of Rukmiṇī.

devanagari: दौहित्रायानिरुद्धाय पौत्रीं रुक्म्याददाद्धरेः । रोचनां बद्धवैरोऽपि स्वसुः प्रियचिकीर्षया । जानन्नधर्मं तद्यौनं स्नेहपाशानुबन्धनः ॥ २५ ...
english_devnagari: dauhitrāyāniruddhāya pautrīṃ rukmyādadāddhareḥ rocanāṃ baddhavairo jānannadharmaṃ tadyaunaṃ snehapāśānubandhanaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ jñā class 9 verb], [vocative single from √ jñā class 9 verb]

translation: Though still nursing deep hostility to Kṛṣṇa and though aware of the violation of the restrictions of dharma-śāstra[4] in such a matrimonial alliance, Rukmī, with the desire of pleasing his sister to whom he was bound by the ties of affection, gave his grand-daughter Rocanā to Hari’s grandson (Rukmī’s daughter’s son).
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Śrīdhara notes the popular objection that one should not dine with the enemy (dviṣad annaṃ na bhoktavyam) and not the real object of dharma- śāstra which discourages in-breeding by prohibiting cross-cousin marriages. Subodhinī quotes:
mātā pitāmahī yasya tathaiva prapitāmahī /
tisra eka-kule jātāḥ so'bhiśasto nigadyate //

devanagari: तस्मिन्नभ्युदये राजन्रुक्मिणी रामकेशवौ । पुरं भोजकटं जग्मुः साम्बप्रद्युम्नकादयः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: tasminnabhyudaye rājanrukmiṇī rāmakeśavau puraṃ bhojakaṭaṃ jagmuḥ sāmbapradyumnakādayaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: For the marriage ceremony, Rukmiṇī, Balarāma, Kṛṣṇa, Sāmba, Pradyumna and others went to the city of Bhojakaṭa.

devanagari: तस्मिन्निवृत्त उद्वाहे कालिङ्गप्रमुखा नृपाः । दृप्तास्ते रुक्मिणं प्रोचुर्बलमक्षैर्विनिर्जय ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: tasminnivṛtta udvāhe kāliṅgapramukhā nṛpāḥ dṛptāste rukmiṇaṃ procurbalamakṣairvinirjaya
translation: When the marriage ceremony was over, some haughty kings like Dantavakra of Kaliṅga and others urged Rukmī, “Completely vanquish Balarāma in the play of dice”.

devanagari: अनक्षज्ञो ह्ययं राजन्नपि तद्व्यसनं महत् । इत्युक्तो बलमाहूय तेनाक्षैर्रुक्म्यदीव्यत ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: anakṣajño hyayaṃ rājannapi tadvyasanaṃ mahat ityukto balamāhūya tenākṣairrukmyadīvyata

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]
[imperfect active second plural], [imperfect passive third single]

translation: Though ignorant of the game of dice, Balarāma is strongly addicted to it, O king.” Thus advised, Rukmī invited Balarāma to play dice and played at dice.

devanagari: शतं सहस्रमयुतं रामस्तत्राददे पणम् । तं तु रुक्म्यजयत्तत्र कालिङ्गः प्राहसद्बलम् । दन्तान्सन्दर्शयन्नुच्चैर्नामृष्यत्तद्धलायुधः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: śataṃ sahasramayutaṃ rāmastatrādade paṇam taṃ tu rukmyajayattatra kāliṅgaḥ prāhasadbalam dantānsandarśayannuccairnāmṛṣyattaddhalāyudhaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: In that game, Balarāma consecutively staked wagers of one hundred, one thousand, ten thousand (coins of gold) and Rukmī won them all. At that time the king of Kaliṅga laughed loudly at Balarāma, displaying his teeth—(an insult) with which Balarāma, the wielḍer of the plough, could not put up.

devanagari: ततो लक्षं रुक्म्यगृह्णाद्ग्लहं तत्राजयद्बलः । जितवानहमित्याह रुक्मी कैतवमाश्रितः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: tato lakṣaṃ rukmyagṛhṇādglahaṃ tatrājayadbalaḥ jitavānahamityāha rukmī kaitavamāśritaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ji class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ ji class 9 verb]

translation: Then Ṛukmī staked one lakh (coins) as a bet which Balarāma now won. But resorting to deceit, Rukmī asserted, “I am the winner”.

devanagari: मन्युना क्षुभितः श्रीमान्समुद्र इव पर्वणि । जात्यारुणाक्षोऽतिरुषा न्यर्बुदं ग्लहमाददे ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: manyunā kṣubhitaḥ śrīmānsamudra iva parvaṇi jātyāruṇākṣo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṣubh class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṣubh class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ kṣubh class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṣubh class 9 verb]
[imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]

translation: Agitated with anger like the swelling ocean under the full moon, the glorious Balarāma whose eyes were naturally reddish, became fiery with extreme rage and he staked a wager of ten crores (of gold coins).

devanagari: तं चापि जितवान्रामो धर्मेण छलमाश्रितः । रुक्मी जितं मयात्रेमे वदन्तु प्राश्निका इति ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ cāpi jitavānrāmo dharmeṇa chalamāśritaḥ rukmī jitaṃ mayātreme vadantu prāśnikā iti

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ji class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ ji class 9 verb]
[accusative single from √ ji class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ ji class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ ji class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ ji class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ ji class 9 verb], [accusative single from √ ji class 9 verb]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation:
As per rules of that game, Balarāma rightly won that bet. But Rukmī resorted to false pretext and said, “I have won the bet. Let the umpires declare their decision”.
32-A. Padaratnāvalī’s Text:
“So be it” said Dantavakra of Kaliṅga showing his teeth (grinning) at Balarāma. Balarāma boiled with rage but (controlled himself and) observed silence.

devanagari: तदाब्रवीन्नभोवाणी बलेनैव जितो ग्लहः । धर्मतो वचनेनैव रुक्मी वदति वै मृषा ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: tadābravīnnabhovāṇī balenaiva jito glahaḥ dharmato vacanenaiva rukmī vadati vai mṛṣā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ji class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ ji class 9 verb]
[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Thereupon a voice from the heaven declared. “As per rules of the game, the wager is properly won by Balarāma. What Rukmī says is false.”

devanagari: तामनादृत्य वैदर्भो दुष्टराजन्यचोदितः । सङ्कर्षणं परिहसन्बभाषे कालचोदितः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: tāmanādṛtya vaidarbho duṣṭarājanyacoditaḥ saṅkarṣaṇaṃ parihasanbabhāṣe kālacoditaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect middle first single], [aorist middle first single]
[nominative single from √ has class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ has class 1 verb]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Ignoring the declaration from the heaven, Rukmī, being instigated by wicked kings and impelled so by his (impending) death, laughed loudly at Saṅkarṣaṇa (Balarāma) and spoke to him.

devanagari: नैवाक्षकोविदा यूयं गोपाला वनगोचराः । अक्षैर्दीव्यन्ति राजानो बाणैश्च न भवादृशाः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: naivākṣakovidā yūyaṃ gopālā vanagocarāḥ akṣairdīvyanti rājāno bāṇaiśca na bhavādṛśāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ yu class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 9 verb]

[nominative single from √ yu class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ yu class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 6 verb], [nominative single from √ yu class 9 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 9 verb]

translation: “You are after-all cowherds ranging over the forests and not experts at the play of dice. Kings like us play with dice and with arrows (i.e. fight) and not persons (cowherds) like you.”

devanagari: रुक्मिणैवमधिक्षिप्तो राजभिश्चोपहासितः । क्रुद्धः परिघमुद्यम्य जघ्ने तं नृम्णसंसदि ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: rukmiṇaivamadhikṣipto rājabhiścopahāsitaḥ kruddhaḥ parighamudyamya jaghne taṃ nṛmṇasaṃsadi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: When thus humiliated by Ṛukmī and ridiculed by kings, Balarāma flew in rage and taking up an iron bar[5], struck Rukmī down in the assembly of kings.

devanagari: कलिङ्गराजं तरसा गृहीत्वा दशमे पदे । दन्तानपातयत्क्रुद्धो योऽहसद्विवृतैर्द्विजैः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: kaliṅgarājaṃ tarasā gṛhītvā daśame pade dantānapātayatkruddho yo
translation: He vehemently caught hold of the (flying) king of Kaliṅga on the tenth pace and angrily knocked out his teeth exhibiting which he laughed at him.

devanagari: अन्ये निर्भिन्नबाहूरु शिरसो रुधिरोक्षिताः । राजानो दुद्रवुर्भीता बलेन परिघार्दिताः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: anye nirbhinnabāhūru śiraso rudhirokṣitāḥ rājāno dudravurbhītā balena parighārditāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ ukṣ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ ukṣ class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ ukṣ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ ukṣ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ ukṣ class 1 verb]

translation: Other kings with their arms, thighs and heads broken, chopped off and bathed in blood when struck with the iron bar by Balarāma, fled away in terror.

devanagari: निहते रुक्मिणि श्याले नाब्रवीत्साध्वसाधु वा । रक्मिणीबलयो राजन्स्नेहभङ्गभयाद्धरिः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: nihate rukmiṇi śyāle nābravītsādhvasādhu vā rakmiṇībalayo rājansnehabhaṅgabhayāddhariḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: When His brother-in-law Ṛukmī was slain, Hari being afraid of alienating the relation with his queen Rukmiṇī and brother Balarāma, expressed no remark for or against the incident.

devanagari: ततोऽनिरुद्धं सह सूर्यया वरं रथं समारोप्य ययुः कुशस्थलीम् । रामादयो भोजकटाद्दशार्हाः सिद्धाखिलार्था मधुसूदनाश्रयाः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: tato rāmādayo bhojakaṭāddaśārhāḥ siddhākhilārthā madhusūdanāśrayāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [instrumental single from √ sūr class 4 verb], [instrumental single from √ sūr class 10 verb]

translation:
Seating Anirudḍha along with Suryā in an excellent chariot, Balarāma and others from the Dāśārha clan of whom Lord Kṛṣṇa was the mainstay, and who had accomplished all their objects, left Bhojakaṭa and journeyed to Dvārakā (Kuśasthalī).
[back to top]

devanagari: अथैकषष्टितमोऽध्यायः 10. ॥ 61 ॥
english_devnagari: athaikaṣaṣṭitamo
